$comment: forge per-project config. The quality gate is single-sourced from .forge/quality_gate_cmd (preflight AND loadProjectConfig read the sidecar); this file omits testProcess.local for that reason — see $testProcess_comment. The gate is the FAST, deterministic, creds-free OFFLINE unit suite scoped to the fork's net-new packages (release + taskagent) — NOT the live TF_ACC acceptance tests (those need a PAT + are 120m-timeout, so they run as sharp per-WI gates / operator confirmation, never as the project default). This pairing is the project-scoped fix for the 2026-05-23 dogfood false-pass (brain: themes/2026-05-23-dogfood-cycle-false-pass-gate.md).
$testProcess_comment: R1-03-F1: the typed test-process contract object. Schema marks testProcess.local required, but THIS file intentionally OMITS it: the fast per-WI gate is single-sourced from the `.forge/quality_gate_cmd` sidecar (`go test -tags all -count=1 ./azuredevops/internal/service/servicehook/...`). loadProjectConfig folds the sidecar into testProcess.local.cmd before validation, so the merged/loaded config always has it — mdtoc (the other example) shows the inline `local` form instead. `ci` is the full CI verification (mirrors GitHub golint.yml: gofmt-check + whole-module `go test` + golangci-lint v2 + terrafmt-check), with `unsetEnv: ["TF_ACC"]` stripping the live-test trigger so the CI mirror never runs the whole live suite (contract A3). `acceptance` is the live-acceptance tier (contract C7): `required` is ADVISORY (false) — the PM adds a live-acceptance WI only when the initiative actually ships or changes live ADO behaviour; `requiresEnv` lists every var the acceptance test's PreCheck demands, so a WI whose gate matches `match` but lacks one of these ERRORS instead of silently skipping (`ok pkg 0.00s`).

cmd: make test && golangci-lint run ./azuredevops/... && make terrafmt-check

northStar: Feature complete ADO provider that has data and resources defined for all resources available in the ADO API
instructions: Build and test only the package you're changing (`go test -tags all ./azuredevops/internal/service/<pkg>/...`); the whole-tree `go build ./...` / `go vet ./...` fill the drive. Make the implementation satisfy the tests as written, and write fixtures with non-default values asserted via a read-back. Commit your work; leave git history intact (no resets). The Release API lives on vsrm.dev.azure.com; in Read, treat a 404 as deleted by clearing the id. Project-action skills live under `forge/skills/`; record each initiative's plan + demo under `forge/history/<initiative-id>/` so the repo carries a browsable build record.
$standing_work_item_acs_comment: Static testing invariants forge appends to EVERY work item (contract A2). Encodes the two-gate model as standing ACs so it no longer depends on per-WI PM judgment. The dev-loop must satisfy both; the reviewer must confirm both.
standing_work_item_acs: Live acceptance: the change is exercised by a TF_ACC acceptance test against real ADO — `terraform apply` → assert the object via the provider read → mandatory idempotency re-plan (`ExpectNonEmptyPlan: false`) → clean `destroy`. New resource/data-source ⇒ a new `TestAcc*`; change to an existing one ⇒ an updated `TestAcc*`. The WI that proves this change MUST set its `testProcess.local.cmd` to the LIVE acceptance command (`go test -tags all -run TestAcc<Name> ./azuredevops/internal/acceptancetests/`, which runs live via the serve env's TF_ACC) — an offline gomock gate cannot catch live-only failures (e.g. ConfigMode:Attr making nested attrs required at apply).

Live evidence: during the acceptance test's live read-back (before destroy), call `_ = testutils.CaptureLiveEvidence(label, url, apiResponse)` (template: `resource_task_group_test.go` `captureTaskGroupEvidence`) so `.forge/live-evidence/<label>.json` is written and `forge demo render` back-fills it into `demo.json`. Use label `acceptance-resource-<type>` — one DISTINCT label per resource type (a shared label means each capture OVERWRITES the previous; the demo compiler picks up every .forge/live-evidence/*.json, so per-type labels lose nothing). For release resources build the vsrm-host GET URL with the release client. The cycle's `demo.json` MUST end with a checkpoint carrying `liveEvidence.url` (a real REST GET), not a test-name table.

CI-equivalent (push-green): the checks forge runs locally must pass — `make test` (gofmt + whole-module `go test`, NO TF_ACC) + golangci-lint v2 incremental (`golangci-lint run --new-from-rev=main ./azuredevops/...`, gating only the code this cycle changes) + `make terrafmt-check`. Keep changed code golangci-clean; do NOT try to fix the ~437 pre-existing upstream staticcheck findings v2 surfaces (separate debt, out of scope). GitHub CI golint.yml mirrors this (golangci v2 + only-new-issues:true). The production resource `.go` carries NO build tag; only `_test.go` files carry `//go:build`; `provider_test.go`'s resource-count list includes any new resource.

Registry docs current: a schema change is NOT done until the published Terraform docs describe it. Run `make docs` (tfplugindocs) so `docs/resources/<resource>.md` + `docs/data-sources/*` document EVERY new/changed attribute, then `git checkout -- docs/guides/` (tfplugindocs deletes the hand-written guides — restore them). Add/refresh the `examples/resources/<resource>/resource.tf` example that the docs embed. Do this BEFORE the PR is opened.
name: terraform-provider-betterado

demoProcess.text: Project tests green in CI after merge

$artifactRoot_comment: Committed forge artifacts (Brain 3 + development history) live under forge/ — a single visible home, out of the provider source tree. forge/brain/ is Brain 3; forge/history/<initiative-id>/ holds each cycle's plan + demo. Runtime scratch (_architect/, AGENT.md, worktree demo/) stays gitignored.
$releaseProcess_comment: Translates CLAUDE.md 'Release — publish after a PR merges to main'. In-cycle: regenerate registry docs (make docs) + author the changelog DRAFT. Pre-merge: bump PROVIDER_VERSION.txt (semver). Tag + GoReleaser publish stay in CI (.github/workflows/tag-on-changelog.yml tags on the finalised CHANGELOG push to main; release.yml runs GoReleaser on the tag) — forge NEVER runs tag/publish.

steps.text: Regenerate the Terraform Registry docs from the schema so docs/resources/ + docs/data-sources/ describe every new/changed attribute, then restore the hand-written guides tfplugindocs deletes. Refresh the examples/ the docs embed.

Add a DRAFT changelog entry under ## Unreleased — one bullet per user-visible behaviour change, categorised (FEATURES / ENHANCEMENTS / BUG FIXES / NOTES). The finaliser promotes it to a versioned section post-approval.
Bump PROVIDER_VERSION.txt (semver: minor for new features/resources/attributes, patch for fixes). The tag-on-changelog CI workflow reads this file to cut the v<version> tag on merge to main.
